description: The figure comprises two scatter plots comparing the cryogenic mechanical properties of the studied Fe-35Mn-6Al-0.1C low-carbon low-density steel (marked as "Present work" with red circles and arrows) against various reported alloys at liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT). Subfigure (a) plots Charpy impact energy (J) versus specific yield strength (MPa·cm³·g⁻¹), while subfigure (b) displays total elongation (%) versus specific yield strength. The data points represent diverse material systems including Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels (Fe35Mn6Al0.1C, Fe30Mn5Al0.3C, Fe28Mn5Al1C, etc.), high-carbon lightweight steels, TWIP steels, 304 stainless steel, 9Ni steel, and Al0.1CoCrFeNi high-entropy alloy. The "Present work" data point, located at approximately 70 MPa·cm³·g⁻¹ specific yield strength, demonstrates superior combinations of high impact toughness (~254 J) and excellent ductility (~75% elongation) compared to most reference materials. Specifically, it exhibits higher impact energy than high-carbon lightweight steels and TWIP steels, superior specific yield strength compared to 304 stainless steel and low-carbon high-manganese steels, and significantly better ductility (nearly 3×) than 9Ni steel. This comparison establishes that the designed steel achieves an outstanding balance of cryogenic strength, toughness, and ductility with reduced density and cost, positioning it as a promising candidate for cryogenic applications.
